description: Redesign mule cards as portrait character cards with grid layout and side drawer

userStories.title: Set up Vitest and add tests for formatMeso
userStories.description: As a developer, I want Vitest configured and formatMeso tested so that I can safely refactor and build new features with confidence.
acceptanceCriteria: Vitest is installed and npm run test works
formatMeso(18000000000, true) returns '18B'
formatMeso(4410000, true) returns '4.41M'
formatMeso(1000000, true) returns '1M'
formatMeso(500000, true) returns '500K'
formatMeso(4840000, true) returns '4.84M' (trailing zero removed)
formatMeso(18000000000, false) returns '18,000,000,000'
formatMeso(4840000, false) returns '4,840,000'
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add tests for calculatePotentialIncome
userStories.description: As a developer, I want calculatePotentialIncome tested so that the core income calculation is verified before refactoring the UI.
acceptanceCriteria: calculatePotentialIncome([]) returns 0
calculatePotentialIncome(['hard-lucid']) returns 504000000
calculatePotentialIncome(['hard-lucid', 'hard-will']) returns sum of both crystal values
calculatePotentialIncome with unknown boss ID ignores it and sums rest
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Extract selectBoss pure function with tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the one-per-family boss selection logic extracted as a pure testable function so that BossCheckboxList can use it without duplicating logic.
acceptanceCriteria: selectBoss([], 'hard-lucid', 'lucid') returns ['hard-lucid'] (add to empty family)
selectBoss(['normal-lucid'], 'hard-lucid', 'lucid') returns ['hard-lucid'] (auto-replace in same family)
selectBoss(['hard-lucid'], 'hard-lucid', 'lucid') returns [] (toggle off / deselect)
selectBoss(['hard-lucid'], 'hard-will', 'will') returns ['hard-lucid', 'hard-will'] (different family, no conflict)
selectBoss(['normal-will', 'hard-lucid'], 'hard-will', 'will') returns ['hard-will', 'hard-lucid'] (replace will, keep lucid)
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add placeholder avatar asset
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a placeholder PNG in src/assets/ so that portrait cards can render an avatar image immediately.
acceptanceCriteria: placeholder.png exists in src/assets/
File is a valid PNG image
Image depicts a generic character silhouette (not blank)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Build MuleCharacterCard component
userStories.description: As a player, I want each mule displayed as a portrait card with avatar, name, level, class, and potential income so that I can identify mules at a glance.
acceptanceCriteria: Card renders at ~200x300px with 2:3 aspect ratio
Top ~60% shows placeholder avatar image
Below avatar: mule name (truncated if long), compact 'Lv. X | Class' line, bold potential income
Card has no drag handle icon (full card is the drag surface)
Card accepts onClick callback prop for opening drawer
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Depends on US-004 for placeholder asset

userStories.title: Build BossCheckboxList component
userStories.description: As a player, I want a searchable boss checklist with checkboxes that enforce one-per-family so that I can select and deselect bosses easily.
acceptanceCriteria: Renders searchable list of boss families
Each family shows checkboxes (not radio buttons) for each difficulty
Search filters by family name and boss name
Checking a boss in a family with an existing selection auto-replaces it using selectBoss function
Unchecking a boss deselects it entirely
Only one boss per family can be checked at a time
Each checkbox label shows boss name and crystal value
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Depends on US-003 for selectBoss function

userStories.title: Build MuleDetailDrawer component
userStories.description: As a player, I want a side drawer that opens from the right with dimmed background so that I can edit mule details without disrupting the grid.
acceptanceCriteria: Drawer opens from the right, ~550px wide, with dimmed overlay
Top of drawer shows character avatar + name + level + class + income header
Below header: name (TextInput), level (NumberInput), class (TextInput) edit fields
Below edit form: BossCheckboxList for boss selection
At bottom: delete button with confirmation step (Alert with Yes/Cancel)
Drawer accepts mule data and CRUD callbacks (onUpdate, onDelete) as props
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Depends on US-005 for avatar header, US-006 for BossCheckboxList

userStories.title: Replace vertical list with responsive grid layout
userStories.description: As a player, I want mule cards in a wrapping grid so that multiple cards are visible at once and the layout is responsive.
acceptanceCriteria: App layout uses Mantine SimpleGrid instead of Stack
Responsive columns: 4 on xl, 3 on lg, 2 on md, 1 on sm
Remove income-based sort logic (sortedMules) — display mules in stored order
Remove restrictToVerticalAxis modifier from DndContext
Use rectSortingStrategy instead of verticalListSortingStrategy
Use PointerSensor with activationConstraint { distance: 5 } on DndContext
expandedMuleId state replaced with selectedMuleId (drives drawer open/close)
Old MuleCard, SortableMuleCard, BossChecklist components removed
pie chart slice click sets selectedMuleId to open drawer
MuleCharacterCard instances render in the grid with useSortable
npm run build passes with no errors
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: This is the integration slice. Depends on US-005, US-006, US-007. Removes old components and wires everything together.

userStories.title: Fix Header to use formatMeso consistently
userStories.description: As a player, I want the header income display to use the same formatting as the rest of the app so that the display is consistent.
acceptanceCriteria: Header uses formatMeso utility instead of raw toLocaleString
Header income display obeys the abbreviated/full toggle state
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
